[164:50]So this is how I would treat this subject

[164:54]because it is important to distinguish.

[164:58]I'm not anti-war at all.

[165:01]War is a necessary extension of statecraft.

[165:07]There's politics, diplomacy, statecraft, and there is war.

[165:11]and interventions of many different kinds

[165:15]and on different levels, it's necessary,

[165:19]especially for a global empire.

[165:22]So I'm not one of these people that says

[165:24]that we will ever achieve a world order

[165:28]without armed conflict of any stripe.

[165:32]So I'm not anti-war for moral or ethical reasons.

[165:36]I think war is frequently necessary.

[165:39]I'm not against interventions.

[165:41]I think that in many cases, again, as the global hegemon,

[165:46]it is necessary for us to intervene in certain instances.

[165:52]So I'm not ideologically against war.

[165:55]I'm not ideologically against intervention.

[165:59]And I would say that my real foreign policy,

[166:03]my real ideology when it comes to matters like this,

[166:07]Is it has to be in the furtherance of America's self-interest, always in every case?

[166:17]It should be just, it should be humane.

[166:19]I believe that we should exhaust diplomatic channels first.

[166:24]And as a last resort in areas concerning vital strategic interests of the American nation,

[166:30]of the American people, I think that interventions up to and including war is sometimes necessary.

[166:37]So I want to make that clear.

[166:38]And in instances like this, we're reminded of why that's an important qualification.

[166:45]Why do we oppose the war in Iraq?

[166:51]Is that the best use of American personnel?

[166:55]Is that the best use of American equipment, resources, money, effort, and energy?

[167:03]Is the best use of American equipment and all the rest of it,

[167:09]fighting a counterinsurgency in Iraq,

[167:13]trying to build up some democratic regime?

[167:16]I think absolutely not.

[167:21]I don't think that was the best use of all the available tools at our disposal to use

[167:27]in that situation.

[167:28]I think that could have been managed.

[167:30]I think there were other ways that we could have went about it.

[167:35]And when you look at a conflict like that, what is obvious is that it's so far away.

[167:41]It's an alien culture.

[167:43]It's a small country, not even a middle power.

[167:51]And you look at who benefits the most, it's certainly not us, it is regional powers in

[167:56]that country.

[167:57]It's countries like Saudi Arabia or Israel or Iran.

[168:01]They all benefit on some level from that far more directly and I think in greater measure

[168:07]than we do.
